title: mespilus monogyna var. hirsuta
intro: Mespilus monogyna var. hirsuta, also known as hirsute medlar, is a variety of the whitebeam tree native to Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The hirsute medlar has been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. The plant is traditionally prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture for use in herbal medicine, and its leaves and bark are often used in folk remedies.
